title: Scyphanthus cumingii
intro: Scyphanthus cumingii, also known as the Philippine scyphanthus, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Asclepiadaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its reported antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Scyphanthus cumingii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is made into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Scyphanthus cumingii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asclepiadaceae. More specifically, it belongs to the genus Scyphanthus. Asclepiadaceae is a family of plants commonly known as the milkweed family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other organisms. Scyphanthus cumingii is native to the Philippines.

benefits: Scyphanthus cumingii is a plant species known to have various health benefits, although information on its specific uses is limited. constituents: Scyphanthus cumingii contains flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids as its active constituents. These compounds have been identified in various studies, contributing to the plant's potential medicinal properties. Flavonoids, in particular, are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Terpenoids have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal activities. The combination of these active constituents in Scyphanthus cumingii may provide a range of health benefits.
